messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0117",
  "title": "休闲手游 热门创意策略",
  "page_index": 62,
  "page_range": "62"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0118",
    "title": "AI搞怪开头+短视频病毒meme+热梗化用",
    "page_range": "62-63",
    "summary": "This section answers retrieval queries regarding 2025 casual mobile game advertising strategies, creative workflows, and performance metrics. **Topics & Procedures:** Details the adoption of AI-generated quirky openings, viral short video memes, and trending internet tropes, outlining standard creative workflows: \"AI opening → actual gameplay → gameplay showcase\" and \"viral video opening → in-game recreation → gameplay showcase.\" **Entities & Genres:** Covers casual mobile games, popular mechanics (parkour, jumping, matching, water sorting), and top-performing puzzle genres (Mahjong connect, Sudoku, word puzzles, solitaire). References data platforms GuangdaDa and TikTok. **Dates:** Analyzes trends from January to December 2025. **Claims & Trends:** Asserts that quirky/entertaining content is becoming the core standard for ad openings, with future creatives increasingly sourced directly from TikTok trends or creator videos. Claims video creatives significantly outperform other formats in impression share for top puzzle games. **Metrics & Figures:** Features two monthly trend charts tracking: (1) advertiser volume (~7.0K–10.0K) and the percentage of advertisers launching new creatives (~76.9%–86.8%), and (2) monthly average active creatives (0–300) and new creative percentages (~43.4%–53.5%). **Evidence Types:** Sourced from GuangdaDa’s Ad Creative and Advertiser Analysis modules, utilizing platform analytics, trend forecasting, and impression share benchmarking."
  }
]

message.content: This section details popular creative strategies for casual mobile game advertising throughout 2025, focusing on AI-generated quirky openings, viral short video memes, and the adaptation of trending internet tropes. It outlines standard creative procedures, specifically the "AI opening → actual gameplay → gameplay showcase" and "viral video opening → in-game recreation → gameplay showcase" workflows. The analysis covers January to December 2025 and references entities including casual mobile games, popular mechanics (parkour, jumping, matching, water sorting), top puzzle genres (Mahjong connect, Sudoku, word puzzles, solitaire), and data platforms GuangdaDa and TikTok. Key claims assert that entertaining content has become the core standard for ad openings, future creatives will increasingly source directly from TikTok trends or creator videos, and video creatives significantly outperform other formats in impression share for leading puzzle games. Metrics are visualized in two monthly trend figures tracking advertiser volume (~7.0K–10.0K) and new creative launch rates (~76.9%–86.8%), alongside average active creatives (0–300) and new creative percentages (~43.4%–53.5%). Evidence is sourced from GuangdaDa’s Ad Creative and Advertiser Analysis modules, utilizing platform analytics, trend forecasting, and impression share benchmarking.

response_text: This section details popular creative strategies for casual mobile game advertising throughout 2025, focusing on AI-generated quirky openings, viral short video memes, and the adaptation of trending internet tropes. It outlines standard creative procedures, specifically the "AI opening → actual gameplay → gameplay showcase" and "viral video opening → in-game recreation → gameplay showcase" workflows. The analysis covers January to December 2025 and references entities including casual mobile games, popular mechanics (parkour, jumping, matching, water sorting), top puzzle genres (Mahjong connect, Sudoku, word puzzles, solitaire), and data platforms GuangdaDa and TikTok. Key claims assert that entertaining content has become the core standard for ad openings, future creatives will increasingly source directly from TikTok trends or creator videos, and video creatives significantly outperform other formats in impression share for leading puzzle games. Metrics are visualized in two monthly trend figures tracking advertiser volume (~7.0K–10.0K) and new creative launch rates (~76.9%–86.8%), alongside average active creatives (0–300) and new creative percentages (~43.4%–53.5%). Evidence is sourced from GuangdaDa’s Ad Creative and Advertiser Analysis modules, utilizing platform analytics, trend forecasting, and impression share benchmarking.
